Residence Hall Council
File Edit View Help

Jester East Residence Hall Council

Year-End Summary Report - 2025–2026 Academic Year · The University of Texas at Austin

Council structure at a glance

Executive Board 12 officers, led by President Mica Fox · primary planners and decision-makers.
Floor Representatives 11 reps · full general-assembly members with secondary committee assignments.
Assembly Members 17 committee members · full GA voice; contributed across the standing committees.
URHA Liaisons Kyle Pham (Board Buddy · URHA Director of Finance) plus rotating spring proxies.
Professional & Student Staff ACC advisor Katres + Senior RA / RA Liaisons (Jaylen, Thang, Sam).

Standing committees

C3 Community Corner Committee

Mondays 6:30 p.m. · Chairs Keira & Zoe · ran the JE Lobby bulletin boards, Community Corner stocks, and the Little Free Library.

TPC Tampons, Pads, Contraceptives

Wednesdays 3 p.m. · Chair Erica · refilled 39 hygiene baskets across floors 1–10 biweekly.

Social Media JEast Social Media

Mondays · Chair Alejandra · merged with RA Social Collateral on Feb 27.

Programming Programming Committee

Floating committee · supported the year’s 30+ events across fall and spring.

If you ever need ANY advice...
✉

I have been involved with RHC in every capacity from member, to president, to RA Liaison, to Advisor. My email is katresai@icloud.com and my number is 442-799-8771 and I would love to hear from you if you need anything!!

Best of luck, Jeast on top!!

Transition Reports

Role-specific knowledge transfers for incoming staff.
President
Vice President
Director of Marketing Transition Letter

Congrats on becoming director of Marketing of Jester East Residence Hall! I hope you are proud of yourself for getting this role. In this role not only will you be able to create social media content for the residence hall but you also will have the duty of leading the Jester East social media committee.

In your role it is necessary to always keep up with the latest social media trends, so your content is relatable to your residents. I would suggest keeping a social media calendar and a folder of everything you guys publish. Some fun ideas we had this year was council member instagram takeover, movie inspired reels, and informative resource posts.

In this position the most important thing is to have fun! Enjoy your time with the social media committee and the residence hall council. I wish you the best and good luck!

— Alejandra

Director of Outreach Transition Letter

First of all, congratulations on being selected to serve as the Director of Outreach for this year’s Jester East Residence Hall Council! This position is a great opportunity to connect with people across the residence hall community and help make RHC feel more involved, collaborative, and welcoming.

As Director of Outreach, your main role is to work with outside residence halls and organizations to plan programs, share ideas, and help unite the area. You will act as a point of contact for other RHCs when they want to collaborate with Jester East or when Jester East wants to partner with them. This means communication is one of the most important parts of your position. Staying organized and responding in a timely way will make your job much easier.

Like other members of RHC, the Director of Outreach is required to put on at least one program during the year. I recommend choosing a program that naturally connects to your role, such as a collaboration with another residence hall, an area‑wide social event, or something that helps residents meet people outside of their usual floor or building. The best outreach programs are ones that make residents feel like they are part of a larger community.

You will also help supervise the C3, or Community Corner Committee, along with the Historian. This committee is responsible for updating and posting things on the council bulletin board near the front desk and one of the bulletin boards in the first‑floor elevator hallway. Make sure these spaces stay updated, organized, and reflective of what RHC is doing. Bulletin boards may not seem exciting, but they are one of the easiest ways for residents to see who represents them and what events are happening. This is the best part of the job by far. Getting to have the community of your committee and building up the community of the residence hall is amazing, and was always my favorite part of the week.

My biggest piece of advice is to communicate early and often. Outreach depends on relationships, and relationships depend on follow‑through. If you reach out to another RHC or organization, keep track of who you contacted, what they said, and what still needs to happen. If you need help, ask the executive board. You do not have to do everything alone. This position is what you make of it. You can keep it simple, or you can use it to help Jester East become more involved in the larger campus community overall. Be creative, stay organized, and remember that your work helps residents feel more connected to each other.

— Keira

Director of Special Projects Transition Doc
Jeast RHC Sustainability Director Transition Doc

Firstly, congratulations on being selected to serve as the Sustainability Director for this year’s Jester East Residence Hall Council. You’ve clearly demonstrated commitment to the community, and this role allows you to shape the daily experience of hundreds of residents. The main purpose of the Residence Hall Council is to advocate for your fellow residents and your work does that through environmental stewardship, food‑security support, and sustainable community building. Jester East depends on you to help create a living environment where students feel supported, informed, and empowered to make choices that benefit both themselves and the planet.

The Sustainability Director’s purpose is to ensure that Jester East remains a community grounded in responsible resource use, waste reduction, and accessible sustainability education. You serve as the primary point of contact for all sustainability‑related initiatives, and your work often intersects with wellness, community engagement, and student support. You will collaborate closely with the Executive Board, advisors, and campus partners to bring programs to life and to ensure that sustainability is woven into the culture of the hall.

You’ve already served on the council, so you understand the rhythm of meetings, programming, and communication, and this role builds on that foundation. It is important that you stay in consistent communication with the President, Vice President, and your council’s Advisor so that any sustainability updates, needs, or initiatives are included in meeting agendas and planning conversations. You will often be the one providing insight on how events can be made more environmentally responsible or how resources can be used more efficiently. You should also share any information you receive from campus sustainability partners such as the Office of Sustainability, Zero Waste Program, or UT Farm Stand that could benefit residents or support council programming. You are the point person for this portfolio, and the board will rely on you to be available, organized, and knowledgeable.

Mastering Roompact will be important for you as well, since you will use it to submit event proposals, track supplies, and coordinate with advisors. You are responsible for making sure that funding requests and events planned are feasible and this applies directly to your role. You will often be the one determining whether a sustainability initiative is realistic, whether supplies are available, and whether the event aligns with Housing & Dining guidelines. Jester East has the resources to support meaningful sustainability programming, and those resources should be used generously when they directly benefit residents and promote responsible living.

As Sustainability Director, one of your most important responsibilities is leading sustainability‑focused programs. This includes food‑waste reduction efforts, resource‑sharing initiatives, educational campaigns, and at least one major sustainability event each year. You may run programs like leftover food redistribution, composting education, clothing swaps, hygiene kit distributions, or sustainable living fairs. You will also maintain inventory of sustainability supplies and ensure that materials are used responsibly and replenished when needed. Your work directly affects residents’ quality of life, especially those who rely on resource‑access programs.

Delegation is essential. While you lead the sustainability portfolio, you are not expected to do everything alone. Work with committee members, collaborate with other halls or organizations, and rely on volunteers when needed. Everyone on the board is capable of fulfilling their responsibilities, and your job is to guide, not to carry the entire load. You will develop your own leadership style, and you should trust yourself as you grow into the role. As long as you are doing what you believe is best for the residents of Jester East, you can be confident that you are doing the job well.

— Vince

Jester East Finance Transition Document
Floor Representative Transition Document

I’m very excited that you’ll be joining the Jester East Residence Hall Council this academic year! It is a great way to get involved on campus and be part of a team that strives to build a welcoming community.

As a Floor Representative, you must be present for weekly council meetings. It’s understandable if you are busy, but you are required to attend a meeting at least every other week. These meetings are extremely important as they offer a space for you to bring your own ideas on how you can help promote engagement in the community and to stay in the loop of everything happening in Jester East Residence Hall Council. It’s also the time when you’d bring floor‑specific concerns or vote on certain policies.

As a floor representative, you are able to help out with events happening in Jester East. It’s all dependent on how you choose to stay involved. I’d say that a key and integral part of being in the Jester East Residence Hall Council is to be present always, especially for your fellow council members. Be willing to help out and attend events they put on, as so much effort and thought is put into making them happen.

Whilst helping with these events, you gain a lot of time management skills, interpersonal skills, and adaptability skills. It’s especially gratifying to see residents enjoy these events and continuously attend them, leaving with a smile on their faces every single time. This is a very meaningful position, and it is truly what you make of it.

— Carmen

Professional Staff

Standing Committees

C3, TPC, and Social Media - year-in-review by committee.

The council operated three primary standing committees, each with chairs and dedicated meeting cadences, plus a Programming committee. Committee members were considered general-assembly members.

C3 - Community Corner Committee

Chairs: Keira and Zoe. Cadence: Mondays at 6:30 p.m. (some sessions extended to 5:30 p.m.). The committee was responsible for the Community Corner, the JE Lobby bulletin boards, and resident-facing handouts.

Monthly bulletin-board themes

MonthTheme
OctoberStar Wars
NovemberFall Snoopy / Peanuts
DecemberPolar Express / sugar cookies
January“Dumb Ways To Dorm” (rules refresher)
FebruaryJellycat “Jedding”
MarchCat in the Hat (“Oh, the places you’ll go”)
AprilThe Amazing World of Gumball
MayHigh School Musicals / Nostalgia / World Travel / Friends

Community Corner stocks

C3 stocked the Community Corner with:

  • Hygiene: toothbrushes, hand sanitizer, deodorant, hand soap, toothpaste
  • Study materials: pencils, pens, post-its, notecards, highlighters, notebook paper, erasers
  • Hair accessories: hair ties, bobby pins, clear elastics, mini scrunchies, combs, brushes, hair clips
  • Small fidget toys: pop-its, squishies, fidget spinners
  • Little Free Library bookshelf built and stocked in November

C3 launch & setup

C3 launched on September 29, 2025 with Keira and Zoe as chairs. From the 10/06/25 C3 meeting, the committee finalized a procurement strategy: “Buy less at the beginning of year, then see how people use items. Prioritize hygiene, school supplies, hair stuff, then fidgets.” The committee voted on and approved C3’s $322.28 Amazon Purchase Order on October 17, executed on December 10.

C3 Purchase Order ($322.28 Amazon, Dec 10)

CategoryItems
HygieneHand Sanitizer ($14.89), Deodorant ($69.95)
HairThick Hair Ties ($5.96), Regular Hairties ($9.99), Bobby Pins ($5.99), Clear Elastics ($6.68), Combs ($18.99), Hair Clips ($5.90)
StudyPencils ($18.99), Pens ($31.99), Big Post-It Notes ($33.99), Notecards ($33.99), Highlighters ($25.99), Erasers ($21.99)
DecorPumpkin Hanging Ornament ($16.99)

Spring projects

  • Building & filling the Little Library bookshelf (Nov 15)
  • Reaching out to Half-Priced Books (no response) and the public library
  • Emailing residents about resources (Wednesdays)
  • Compiling Sure Walk, UT Lyft, parking-garage helpline, Longhorn Auto Assistance Program (LAAP) info
  • Creating a “Brazos” board
  • Updating the board and Community Corner
  • Redoing the library
  • “Meet the Council” board following the Star Wars theme

TPC - Tampons, Pads, Contraceptives

HAC - Hygiene Access Committee (recommended rebrand). Chair: Erica (built a TPC request form and Jester layout maps). Cadence: Wednesdays at 3 p.m. through the spring (originally first Thursdays).

The committee assembled, distributed, and biweekly-refilled hygiene baskets in every community bathroom on floors 1–10 of Jester East - 39 baskets total per the refill volunteer roster. Each basket carried a QR code that linked to a request form; when residents requested refills, a TPC volunteer placed the requested product in a plastic bag and dropped it in the appropriate RA’s basket originally, but due to ineffectiveness they did distribution themselves in the spring.

Standard basket contents (per the 10/02/25 TPC meeting)

ItemPer basketTotal across 39 baskets
Liners10150
Condoms (men’s bathrooms)10225 combined
Condoms (women’s bathrooms)5(see above)
Tampons10150
Pads575

The committee also assembled “Jessence” poopourri spray for distribution and decorated TPC aprons in March/April. Condoms were provided from the Longhorn Wellness Center. Budget: ~$200 every two weeks for refills.

Year timeline

DateMilestone
Oct 2Setup; future meetings became first Thursday of every month. QR codes laminated and attached to baskets.
Oct 17Distribution started; more liners/tampons/condoms ordered. Keira submitted an additional condom order.
Oct 249th and 10th floors completed. Need for more condoms identified.
Oct 31TPC making Jessence kits, distributed bags to RAs to fill.
Nov 5TPC Purchase Order: $173.88 (Amazon Basics regular pads, Stayfree Maxi 66-pk ×2, U by Kotex Click Compact tampons ×3, Dr. Emma mini disposable toothbrushes with floss ×2).
Nov 7“Going to finish jessence soon, waiting on condoms to make the baggies.”
Nov 14Made baggies for distribution.
Feb 2TPC Committee Purchase Order: $164.64 (Amazon Basics pantiliners 272-ct ×2, Tampax Radiant tampons multipack ×3, NETANY plastic storage baskets, NaturalCozy 5-piece woven baskets).
Feb 5All bathrooms refilled; baskets stored in Katres’ office.
Feb 19Refills complete except men’s bathroom (waiting on condoms); new baskets received; aprons in pipeline.
Feb 26Condoms received, all stocked. Aprons due in 2 weeks; plan to bedazzle aprons (TPC trademark). Refill scheduled for March 11. Julian needs help.
Mar 5Refills finished; two new candidates interested. March 11: decorate aprons. No condoms in boys’ bathroom; refilled next cycle.
Mar 12Distribution as planned, no major updates.
Mar 26Aprons started; decorating planned next Wednesday. Distribution successful; more condoms needed again.
Apr 2Apron decorating next Wednesday.
Apr 9Decorated aprons completed.

Social Media - JEast Social Media Team

Chair: Alejandra. Met regularly through the year. Started fall with brainstorming a posting schedule and ideas: 1 post/week + Instagram polls + Instagram takeovers.

On Feb 27, the committee merged with the RA Social Media Collateral team to become the unified JEast Social Media Team. Alejandra also took on writing the monthly newsletter beginning September 26.

Major posts & reels

  • First reel posted Oct 17
  • Snoopy themed posts in November
  • KPDH (Kpop Demon Hunters) promotion reel
  • Resource booklet filming video in October/November
  • Jester Formal (Jormal) recap posts
  • “2016 post” (highly successful - “2016 post ate down”)
  • Lucky Charms + Mar10 (Mario) recap posts
  • Twilight content posted closer to Twilight Marathon
  • Move-out themed TikToks (April): How to defrost fridge tutorial, ASMR slime making
  • Newsletter sent April 2 (Jedding love story promotion soon)
  • Spring break dining hall hours post queued

Programs & Events

A chronological catalog of every program the RHC executed or supported during the year.

Fall 2025 Semester

Aloe! From Hall Council - August 27, 2025

The kickoff event of the year. An info session for the RHC held in the Jester West Lobby at 6 p.m. Residents received snacks, succulents, treats, fidgets, and wellness kits while learning about hall council and how to join.

  • Leads: Katres, Rotem
  • Setup/During/Takedown: Thang, Jaylen, Colin, Gibby, Jake
  • Campus Partner: Jester West RHC
  • Cost: $563.50 · Attendance: 120 people
  • Items purchased (Amazon, total $563.50): Tillandsia Air Plants Live (75 Pack) ×4, 10 lb succulent/cactus pebbles, 24-pc small mini clay pots ×2, That’s It Fun Size Fruit Bars Variety ×2, SkinnyPop Original Popcorn 30ct ×3, Nature Valley Crunchy Granola Bars 60-ct ×2, Stash Tea Fruity Herbal 6-flavor ×2, Mueller Vegetable Chopper, ZHANG XIAO QUAN 5-in-1 (knife/scissor set), ZENY 3-Step Folding Ladder. (Purchaser: Rotem Cohen.)

Jester United / RHC Retreat - October 19, 2025

Joint East/West retreat at the JE Red River Lounge from 4–7 p.m. (“secret :p it’ll be fun!”). Catered food, bonding, games, and prodevo (professional development) presentations from advisors. Attendance: 20.

  • Leads: Katres, Rotem (with RA Liaisons assisting setup/during/takedown)
  • Cost: $443.10 (Purchaser: Rotem Cohen). Vendors: Potbelly, HEB, Tiff’s Treats.
  • Catering: 4 Box O’ Sandwiches for 10 ($295.16 from Potbelly), HEB chip variety packs and family fun mix, HEB Twist/Dr. B/Cola sodas, Capri Sun 30-pack variety, Tiff’s Mix 3.5 dozen cookies + delivery fee.

HallowEast Movie Night (Coraline) - October 24, 2025

Held in the Red River Lounge, 7:45 snacks / 8 p.m. movie / ~10 end. Showed Coraline (with the option to swap) followed by a fun Kahoot quiz with a candy prize for the winner. Snacks and drinks provided; residents brought blankets and pillows and came in PJs. ~10 paper flyers were posted.

  • Leads: Julian, Erica (Setup/During/Takedown: Erica, Kayla, Zoe, Julian)
  • Cost: $61.69 · Attendance: 27
  • Target purchases: Sprite 2L ×2, Coca-Cola 2L, Mtn. Dew 2L, Dr. Pepper 2L, Rose’s Grenadine, white frosted sugar cookies ×2, chocolate chip cookies, Frito-Lay Snack Mix 42-ct, Nerds Halloween Spooky Gummy Clusters.

HallowEast / DTI Halloween Edition Costume Contest - October 25, 2025

Trick-or-Treat booths, costume contest, refreshments/snacks, and a costume drawing contest in the Jester Plaza & Breezeway, 1–3 p.m.

  • Leads: Selena, Erica (Setup: Erica, Selena, Kayla, Zoe; During/Takedown: Erica, Selena)
  • Cost: $178.75 · Attendance: 72
  • Items: HEB pumpkin cookies ×4; Target pumpkin baskets ×2, red cups & blue cups (72-ct each), Hawaiian Punch ×3; Amazon M&M full-size candy bars, Halloween blanket ×2, Boo! plush, black cat plush, bulk assorted candies, candy corn.

Rock the Pink - October 28, 2025

A breast-cancer-awareness booth at a URHA-sponsored event in the Quad Courtyard, 7–9 p.m. Activities themed around pink and pink energy/zero-sugar drinks; messaging about breast cancer; “charge up against breast cancer” tagline; mini-golf, photobooth, ring-toss, beer-pong (passport stamps with activities), pink-drink station; wearing pink earned residents an energy drink. Residents were encouraged to post with hashtags.

  • Leads: Mica, Austin (Setup: Keira, Zoe; During: Kayla, Zoe, Keira, Abhishek; Takedown: Brooke, Julian)
  • Campus Partner: URHA (URHA covered the cost - $0 to JE RHC + $511.62 from URHA collaborative fund)
  • Attendance: ~350 residents
  • Items (Amazon, $511.62): table cloth, pink ribbon stickers (600 pcs), cones ring toss, PRIME Hydration Strawberry Watermelon 12-pk ×6, Poppi Soda Raspberry Rose 12-pk ×5, Gatorade Zero Watermelon Splash 12-pk ×14, Red Bull Wild Berries Pink Edition 24-pk ×3.

Jessence Distribution / Purchase Order - October 28, 2025

A separate $72.70 Amazon purchase to assemble “Jessence” hygiene goody bags. Items: Nature’s Oil Witch Hazel 1 gallon, Lemongrass Essential Oil 16 oz, small clear travel spray bottles ×3.

Día de los Muertos - November 2, 2025

An ofrenda was set up next to the Community Corner on the night of October 31 (residents could submit photos via Google Form), followed by a celebration in the JE Lobby on November 2 at 7 p.m. with food, music, and a screening of “Book of Life” in the TV lounge.

  • Lead: Katres (Setup: Zoe, Austin, Brooke, Vince, Medha)
  • Cost: $342 (per event calendar - itemized FRF totaled $274.08 with Amazon $67.97 + HEB $206.11)
  • Attendance: 342
  • Items: Amazon - Mexico Tablecloth 60×104, 82-pc Day of the Dead Altar Kit (LED candles, paper marigolds), rechargeable acrylic flameless votives. HEB - Floral 5-Stem Marigold Bunch ×2, 40-ct Conchas, Mini Pan de Polvo cookies ×3, Texas Tamale Co. Assorted Tamales ×11, Herdez Mild Salsa Verde ×2, HEB Fresh Guacamole ×2, Nestle Abuelita Mexican Hot Chocolate ×3, half-size aluminum pan.

Wicked Movie Night - November 7, 2025

Showing of “Wicked” with popcorn at the Red River Lounge, 7:45 snacks / 8 p.m. start. Setup/help from Kayla, Katres, Austin, Erica, Rayyan, Maggie.

  • Leads: Austin, Kayla; Setup: Kayla, Brooke, Selena, Abhishek
  • Cost: $172.55 · Attendance: 45
  • Items: HEB - Kernel Season’s Movie Theater Butter Oil ×5, pink frosted sugar cookies, sugar cookies 18-pc, butter salt, HEB Cola/Dr. B/Twist/Root Beer 12-pk cans, heavy-duty paper plates 100-ct. Amazon - 48-pc round floor pillows ($95.99).

Kpop Demon Hunters Movie Night - November 15, 2025

Maruchan ramen with assorted toppings while watching Kpop Demon Hunters in the Red River Lounge, 7:45 snacks / 8 p.m. movie.

  • Leads: Mica, Zoe (Setup/During/Takedown: Mica, Kayla)
  • Cost: $90.92 · Attendance: 78
  • Items: HEB - Beef/Chicken/Shrimp Maruchan multipacks (29 packs total), chili oil ×2, lime juice, soy sauce, sriracha, toasted sesame seeds, sliced green onion ×2. Amazon - electric kettles 1.7L ×2 ($28.78).

A Little Library - November 15, 2025

Donated bookshelf installation for the JE Lobby. Funded $96.92 (Amazon): 6-Tier Tree Bookshelf ($34.99), magnetic name tags 15-pack ($21.99), Twinings Herbal Tea Variety Pack 40-ct ($19.95), 5-piece storage basket set ($19.99). Books were sourced for free; the team had been seeking shelving and signage. Bookshelf arrived November 7 and was assembled.

Mega-Mindfulness / Megamind Movie Night - November 19, 2025

Movie night themed around Megamind in the Red River Lounge using the new popcorn machine, 7:45 snacks / 8 p.m. start to ~10 p.m.

  • Leads: Kayla, Abhishek (Setup: Abhishek, Kayla, Keira)
  • Cost: $190.44 · Attendance: 22
  • Items: HEB - mini pretzel sticks, Chex Mix, Doritos Party Size, Capri Sun 30-ct, sour cream & onion / BBQ chips, HEB Twist & Cola 12-ct, napkins 250-ct, bowls 70-ct, Nerds gummy clusters (prize). Amazon - 5-piece storage basket set, prize blanket, Megamind sticker, Orville Redenbacher’s gourmet kernels, popcorn sleeves 800-ct, Nabisco cookie & candy variety, M&M minis, sour bear gummies, Gorilla Super Glue Gel.

Fuel for Finals - November 17, 2025

“Cute café vibes” with a barista experience, custom menu, sandwiches and sweets in the Red River Lounge, 6:30–9 p.m. (until supplies lasted).

  • Leads: Mica, Alejandra (Setup: Kayla, Brooke, Selena, Abhishek)
  • Cost: $427.09 · Attendance: 112
  • Amazon items: pink + aqua gingham tablecloths (3-pack each), fake succulent plants 6-pack ×2, animal stickers 200-pc, ceremonial-grade matcha 3.53 oz, condiment squeeze bottles, Torani brown sugar cinnamon syrup, Torani strawberry syrup, plastic cups w/ sip lids 60-ct ×2, coffee cups w/ lids & sleeves 50-ct, paper plates 100-ct.
  • HEB items: honey bear ×2, ground cinnamon ×2, Nescafe Gold Blonde instant coffee ×2, whole milk gallon ×4, Planet Oat oatmilk 86 oz ×5, finger sandwich tray, sandwich wrap tray, rotisserie chicken sandwiches 24-ct, cookie & brownie bites tray, donut holes tray, loaf cake tray, Arizona Green Tea ×3, large fresh fruit tray ×2, Bigelow Lemon Ginger Tea ×2, peppermint tea ×2, Imperial sugar cubes, paper napkins, Splenda Stevia packets.

Trail of Lights (Jester United) - December 4, 2025

The flagship JesterUnited holiday event at Jester Plaza, with the Jester West RHC. Activities included plaza decorating with holiday lights, an apple-cider and hot-chocolate bar (candy canes, marshmallows), cookie decorating, a holiday photobooth with polaroids, custom snow-globe making, and an ugly sweater contest at the lights launch. A scavenger hunt and a sleigh ride on a university golf cart were proposed. The proposal received attention from “some important folks in UHD” who stopped by and were impressed.

  • Leads: Katres, Alejandra, Keira, Erica (“All” set up and ran during)
  • Campus Partner: Jester West RHC
  • Cost: $985.66 (event calendar lists Cost/Attendance: 372)
  • Attendance: 372 (largest single program of the year)
  • Items: HEB - 300 sugar cookies (custom order, $90), authentic hot chocolate drink tablets 6-pk ×20 ($116.40), Higher Harvest ND Original Avena Milk ×20, Louisburg Cider Mill Old Fashioned Apple Cider ×9. Amazon - INSTAX Mini Instant Film 100-pk ×2 + Fujifilm Instax Mini 12 camera ($249.98 + $89.99), Spangler Red & White Mini Candy Canes 500-ct, mini marshmallows 2 lb bag, ugly Christmas sweater medals 3-pc, 32-pack Christmas plastic snow globes kit ×2 ($75.98), Wilton Christmas cookie icing 3-pack ×3, ECOLipak 500-ct compostable paper cups.

Holiday Movie Night (“Whoville and Hot Cocoa”) - December 5, 2025

Holiday-themed movie with hot cocoa and popcorn in the Red River Lounge, 7:45 snacks / 8 p.m. start. Free to RHC ($0 incremental cost).

  • Leads: Kayla, Vince
  • Cost: $0 · Attendance: 65

Gingerbread House Decorating - December 3, 2025

Held in the Red River Lounge, 5–7 p.m.

  • Leads: Selena, Maggie (Setup: Layla, Vince)
  • Cost: $90 (per event calendar) · Attendance: 90

Cookie Decorating - December 3, 2025

Cookie decorating event paired with the gingerbread theme. Funded $97.74 across HEB and Target.

  • Items: Target - Christmas writing icing ×2, Christmas cookie decorating kit ×3, gingerbread houses ×2, table cloths ×4. HEB - Swiss Miss Marshmallow Mix ×2.

Colors & Connection - December 2, 2025

A creative wellness event in the Red River Lounge from 5 p.m. setup / 6 p.m. event start. Inspired by an Art and Healing project (LinkedIn link cited in the event calendar).

  • Lead: Nicole Wu (Setup: Kayla, Keira)
  • Cost: $374.34 · Attendance: 36
  • Items: Amazon - DEDC LED mini spot lights 4-pc, white beeswax pellets 5 lb, silicone freezer trays ×2 (incl. W&P Cup Cubes Blue), Twinings tea herbal variety, bulk candy mix variety pack ($44.95), 5-pc round small woven basket set, chocolate bulk candy variety ($39.97), Nature Valley granola ×5 ($56.35), ESRICH 4×4″ small canvases ×2, paint brushes & palette set, 24-pack hand cream gift set, Cottonelle flushable wet wipes. Target - Mezcla Puff Bars ×3, stoneware threshold container/bowl set ×3, Fresh Sun Beams Mandarin Oranges.

Ornament Decorating - December 8, 2025

Ornament decorating event in the Red River Lounge at 7:30 p.m.

  • Leads: Navya, Brooke
  • Cost: $97.01 · Attendance: 54
  • Items: Amazon - artificial pine branches 24-pc + red berries 72-pc ×2, clear plastic ornaments 24-ct ×2. Target - Swiss Miss Milk Chocolate Hot Cocoa 8-ct ×3, Holiday Oreos 45-ct ×2.

Spring 2026 Semester

Jester Formal / “Jormal” - January 15, 2026

Jester East and West held a formal in the Jester East Lobby starting 7 p.m. Planning leveraged collaborative funds from URHA. Proposal was “fully approved all the way up to the director level,” presented to URHA Eboard by Mica and Manoj, and received URHA funding. The event won “Best Vibes” award at year-end and was “the first UHD event of 2026” - recognized for forward planning.

  • Campus Partners: Jester West RHC + URHA (funding)
  • Lead: Vince

Jester City Limits (Org Fair) - Tentative late January / moved later

Org fair for student orgs and departments planned for the spring. JesterUnited event with the West RHC. RHC provided food, setup support, and organized a stamp/passport system. Campus Partners: Jester West RHC and student organizations (Pivot Point Central - Henry Kim).

Red River Cinema: Twilight - January 30, 2026

First Red River Cinema Friday-night movie of the spring. The series itself won an OTM award (“Jester East won an OTM award for our Red River Cinema series!”).

  • Leads: Erica, Zoe
  • Cost: $89.61 (HEB + Amazon)
  • Items: HEB - Frito Lay flavor mix chips, apple strudel pastry bites, two-bite brownies ×3, Kool-Aid drink mix (watermelon, black cherry, strawberry, cherry, ×2 each), Sprite/Barq’s/Diet Coke/Coca-Cola 12-can fridge packs, pure cane sugar 2 lb. Amazon - 20-pack blood bags for drinks 8.5 oz ($9.99) - for the vampire theme.

Red River Cinema: 10 Things I Hate About You - February 5, 2026

Second movie night of the spring; happened the same night as the Feb 5 GA meeting.

Bunches of Love - February 10, 2026

Safe-sex education paired with build-your-own mini-bouquet activity in the Jester East Lobby, 7 p.m. Mini bouquets of flowers from HEB (trimmed and made into cones), with chocolate, flower, and origami tutorials.

  • Lead: Keira (Setup: Layla)

Baked with Love - February 11, 2026

Cookie decorating in the Red River Lounge, 4–6 p.m. - heart-shaped cookies and bears, icing, and napkins.

  • Leads: Maggie, Selena
  • Cost: $179.39 (Target) - Valentine’s Teddy Bear Heart cookie kit ×8 ($79.92), Valentine’s Sparkle Heart cookie kit ×8 ($79.92), 4-pack writing icing ×3, rectangular table covers ×4, white paper plates ×2.

Red River Cinema: Scary Movie 4 - February 13, 2026

  • Leads: Erica, Austin

Red River Cinema: Princess Bride - February 20, 2026

  • Lead: Keira

Donation Drive - February 19, 2026

Clothing/food/book donation drive coordinated with East/West sustainability collab. $28.98 spent at HEB on Sterilite Latching Storage Boxes ×3 ($19.50) and fragrance-free detergent pacs.

  • Leads: Vince + Abhishek (and clothing drive deliveries to St. Vincent April 20 – May 1)

Red River Cinema: The Notebook - February 27, 2026

  • Leads: Prisha, Erica, Katres

Red River Cinema: Rio / Pitch Perfect - March 6, 2026

Movie chosen by Instagram poll - Pitch Perfect and Rio were tied; Rio was selected (per the 3.12 agenda).

  • Leads: Alejandra, Keira

Spring Forward - March 9, 2026

Caffeine boost in the Jester East Lobby from 7–10 a.m. to give residents an energy boost after losing an hour to daylight saving time.

  • Leads: Mica, Keira, Vince, Austin
  • Cost: $364.45 (final used FRF, after a $441.92 v1 was scrapped)
  • Items (final): Amazon - pop fidget toys 57-pc ×2, motivational stress balls 100-pc. HEB - Red Bull ×4 ($77.92), Alani Nu ×2, Monster Energy ×2, Bloom ×4 ($79.92), Starbucks Doubleshot Cans ×4, Liquid Death Sweet Tea ×4, HALFDAY Peach Iced Tea ×3.

Lucky Charms - March 11, 2026

Charm-bracelet/key-charm/bookmark making in the Red River Lounge, 6–7 p.m. Leprechaun-themed with Irish pub music, green snacks, Lucky Charms marshmallows, chocolate gold coins, scavenger hunt with rainbow at the end (“follow the rainbow” prize), Bevo template charms. Wear-green encouraged.

  • Lead: Keira (Setup: Leon, Karim, Austin tentative)
  • Cost: $99.58 / $119.55 (two FRFs filed; the $99.58 version was used)
  • Items: Amazon - Kandi Pony Beads 3500-pc, RYKOMO Acrylic Charms 1250-pc, bag charm hooks 100-pc 6-color, chocolate gold coins 1 lb, Lucky Charms cereal bowls 12-pck ×2, Lucky Charms cereal bars 16-pck ×2.

Red River Cinema: Sinners - March 12, 2026

Tied to a small $9.99 Amazon FRF for Halloween Teeth Fangs 36-pc (white) for the vampire-themed movie.

Spring into the Season - March 24, 2026

Air-plant and plant-pot decorating to celebrate the equinox / arrival of spring in the Red River Lounge, 6:30–7:30 p.m.

  • Leads: Alejandra, Mica, Navya

Egg Hunt - March 25, 2026

Egg hunt and spoon relay in the Red River Lounge, 8–9 p.m. 20 participants max, with prizes. Coordinated with URHA’s OTM writing party (which preceded it 6–7 p.m.); URHA helped purchase decorations and set up as thanks for letting events overlap. Praised by Katres at the 3.26 meeting: “Excellent work by Abhishek, Erica, Leon, and Carmen on the Egg Hunt event! Loved the different components and it was a very impressive spread.”

  • Leads: Abhishek, Carmen

Red River Cinema: Shark Tales? - March 27, 2026

  • Leads: Keira, Carmen

Red River Cinema: Bottoms - April 1, 2026 (originally planned April 3rd, moved)

Movie night for Bottoms (planned at $73.59 across Amazon scratch-off movie posters and HEB sodas/snacks; FRF marked “did not use” - likely partially executed).

  • Leads: Mica, Zoe, Carmen

Twilight Marathon (“Twilight Week”) - April 7–10, 2026

A four-day marathon in the Red River Lounge starting Tuesday April 7 with Twilight, then continuing the saga through April 10. Movies started at 7:30 p.m. each night. “A week full of snacks and vampire things.” Erica reached out to URHA for extra funding. Raffle for T-shirts (“Bella, where have you been loca?”).

  • Leads: Navya, Erica
  • Cost: $221.53 (Amazon + HEB + Target)
  • Items: Target - white frosted sprinkles sugar cookies ×2, Twizzlers strawberry, Frito-Lay variety pack ×2, Frito-Lay Flamin’ Hot mix ×2, brownie bites 12-ct, Ghirardelli milk chocolate squares 4.8 oz, gluten-free mini cupcakes. HEB - Country Time Lemonade Drink Mix ×4, Kool-Aid Cherry ×24, apple strudel pastry bites ×2, Italian Cream Cake ($16.98), Twist Lemon-Lime / Dr. B / unsweetened cranberry-raspberry sparkling water 12-pks. Amazon - Twilight “I Love Boys Who Sparkle” T-shirt, Jacob Black “Loca Heart” Twilight T-shirt, sage green scalloped disposable tableware set 175-pc.

Jedding (Jester Wedding) - April 10, 2026

The marquee farewell event of the year - a formal wedding ceremony between Jester East’s phoenix mascot and Jester West’s dragon mascot, held at the Jester Plaza (with Jester Auditorium as a weather backup). The event included a formal ceremony with a reception afterwards: catered pasta from UHD, punch and cake, a photobooth, and a card-writing station for residents to leave advice for future students. Theme: Royalty. Dress: business casual + name tag.

  • Leads: Mica, Austin, Erica, Vince
  • Campus Partner: JW RHC
  • Original date was April 3rd - moved to April 10th by vote
  • Pre-event: Banner painted April 3 at 12 p.m.; floor reps disperse door flyers Monday; potential ceremony walk-through Wed/Thu prior. Vince met with Mark to discuss the altar moment (fist-bump? hug?). Mica coordinated with URHA on photobooth frame.
  • Costume: Phoenix costume sourced (preferably inflatable) - backup plan: parrot decorated as phoenix. Quote secured for UHD pasta catering.
  • Day-of: signup shifts via Teams. Funding requested from URHA on March 30/23. Budget aim: < $1,000 each (East and West) depending on what each side purchased.

Slime & Study - April 15, 2026

Slime-making event in the Red River Lounge from 5:30–6:30 p.m.

  • Leads: Austin, Zoe
  • Cost: $90.57 (Amazon) - 1 gallon all-purpose glue ×2, 8 oz disposable plastic storage containers 40-ct, 12 oz saline solution 2-ct, 14.9 oz shaving cream ×2, 16 oz baking soda, popsicle stirring sticks 100-ct, food coloring (red/yellow/green/blue), essential oils, 8-count disposable plastic table cloth.

Jester Jollapalooza - April 16, 2026

A joint East-West RA-planned event from 7–9 p.m. The RHC sought volunteers; Katres shared a sign-up at the 3.12 meeting.

Student Leadership Celebration - April 14, 2026 (6:30–8 p.m.)

Held at San Jac MPR with food, awards, and NRHH induction (open to everyone). Spring Forward was nominated for an OTM by Austin.

Ponyo & Ramen - April 8, 2026 (Cancelled)

Originally planned for April 8 in the Red River Lounge at 6 p.m. with Katres and Carmen as leads.

Sustainability Event - Changed to Slime Event

A planned sustainability event by Austin and Vince was changed to a slime event during the 3.26 meeting.

Red River Cinema (Movie-Night Series)

Red River Cinema was Jester East’s signature recurring program: weekly Friday-night movie screenings in the Red River Lounge with free popcorn from the new popcorn machine, snacks, and themed décor. The series ran throughout fall and spring. Format: 7:45 snacks, 8 p.m. movie start, ~10 p.m. end.

The series won an OTM (Of the Month) award (“Jester East won an OTM award for our Red River Cinema series!”) and earned kudos from upper UHD leadership. Movies played throughout the year:

DateMovieNotes
Oct 24CoralineHallowEast, $61.69, 27 attendees
Nov 7Wicked$172.55, 45 attendees
Nov 15Kpop Demon Hunters$90.92, 78 attendees
Dec 5Holiday Movie / “Whoville” with hot cocoa$0 incremental, 65 attendees
Jan 23Twilight-
Jan 30Twilight (full cinema-style)-
Feb 510 Things I Hate About You-
Feb 13Scary Movie 4Erica, Austin
Feb 20Princess BrideKeira
Feb 27The NotebookPrisha, Erica, Katres
Mar 6Rio / Pitch PerfectInstagram poll-decided; tied; Rio chosen
Mar 12Sinners$9.99 fangs
Mar 27Shark Tales?Keira, Carmen
Apr 1BottomsFRF prepared, marked “did not use”
Apr 17Camp RockKeira
Apr 24The Devil Wears Prada-

Logistics innovation: at the Oct 24 meeting, Katres proposed weekly free-popcorn movie nights as “low cost / effort and effective community building.” Recommendations like polling residents on Instagram for movie choices were adopted. The new 85-inch 4k Samsung TV (ordered fall 2025) was installed in the TV Lounge and used for the series. The 120″ motorized projector screen for the Red River Lounge required ceiling drilling and director-level approval; in the meantime a collapsible screen was used.

Notable Capital / Equipment Purchases

  • Annual T-shirt order ($2,700) - council apparel ordered for the year.
  • New 85-inch 4K Samsung TV - ordered for the TV Lounge, installed via UT IT (Oct 2025).
  • Aoxun 120-inch Motorized Projector Screen ($149.99) + Command Small Refill Adhesive Strips ($6.40) - for the Red River Lounge “Screen Unveiling” Oct 13, 2025. Installation required ceiling drilling and director-level approval.
  • Farberware Countertop Microwave 1000W 1.3 Cu Ft ($119.99) + HOVER COVER Magnetic Splatter Cover ($19.99) - for the JE kitchen, Oct 15, 2025.
  • Piano-area lock initiative - driven by quiet-hours complaints. Multiple FRFs: Refrigerator Lock 2-pack + Command 20 lb XL Heavyweight Picture Hangers + Hook & Loop Straps with Buckles ($30.97, Nov 3); piano-lock repair ($36.43 in HEB, Feb 20); NeoPRO Generic Piano Lock Key ($18.99, Mar 2). Also a written proposal to build doors over the piano area.
  • Hoctieon 6-Tier Tree Bookshelf for the Little Free Library ($34.99, Nov 15).
  • 48-pc round floor pillows for movie nights ($95.99 - part of Wicked FRF).
  • Fujifilm Instax Mini 12 Instant Film Camera + 100-pk INSTAX Mini Instant Film ×2 for Trail of Lights polaroid station (~$340).
  • RA On-Call Supply purchases: phone chargers 2-pack ($29.97); kitchen supplies - Matter compostable kitchen items ×2, Ohuhu over-the-sink dish drainer, Aux to USB-C cable ($86.96, Feb 2).

URHA / Collaborative Funding Highlights

  • Rock the Pink - URHA covered the entire $511.62 cost of JE RHC’s Amazon items (“$0 + $563.62 URHA” per event calendar).
  • Jester Formal (Jormal) - proposal approved up to director level, presented to URHA Eboard by Mica and Manoj on Wednesday in late fall, received URHA collaborative funding.
  • Twilight Marathon - Erica reached out to URHA for extra funding.
  • Jedding - URHA funding requested March 30/23. Photobooth frame coordinated with URHA.
  • Egg Hunt - URHA helped pay for decorations and helped with setup as thanks for letting events overlap with their OTM writing party.
  • RA programming line - $2,000 allocated for the year (decided at the 9/26 meeting).

Meetings, Attendance, & Governance

Meeting cadence, agenda format, and meeting-by-meeting highlights.

Meeting Cadence

The General Assembly met weekly Friday afternoons in the Jester Area Conference Room (with occasional shifts to the Jester RHC Office).

Standard agenda format

  1. Icebreaker (Kayla)
  2. Advisor Updates (Katres)
  3. Senator Updates (Mica/Austin)
  4. Committee Updates (Alejandra, Zoe, Keira, Erica)
  5. Programming/Events
  6. Notes/Tasks/Kudos

Executive Board (EB) met separately on alternating weeks. The Social Media committee met 4 p.m. Mondays (sometimes shifted to evening), C3 met Mondays 6:30 p.m., and TPC met Wednesdays 3 p.m. (originally first Thursdays). The full GA meeting attendance roster shows 10 fall meetings and 14 spring meetings.

Fall 2025 - Meeting-by-Meeting Highlights

September 19 - Founding meeting

  • Constitution overview and ratification (approved unanimously).
  • Committee structure established: TPC, P3, Social Media, +.
  • Attendance/strikes policy adopted.
  • Discussed Campus Cup competition: “We won last year.”
  • PTC/Jessence distribution and Back-the-Pink event introduced.
  • Rotem and Katres introduced the funding/event-calendar excel.

September 26

  • Reviewed event calendar; allocated $2,000 for RA programming.
  • RHC retreat scheduled October 19 (head count to follow).
  • Towers of Terror volunteer signup (Keira, Kayla, Erica, Mica, Selena).
  • Created monthly email newsletter - Alejandra volunteered.
  • Decision: contribute ~$700 toward new TV + projector/screen.
  • JW RHC asked JE to help fund a PS5; JE offered $250, recommended URHA funding for the rest.
  • NRHH and professional-development workshop information shared.
  • Numerous task assignments: Alejandra → newsletter; Zoe/Keira/C3 → “Meet the Council” board; Erica/TPC → assemble baskets + QR code form; Navya → FRFs for Halloween events; Selena/Erica/Layla → costume contest.

October 3

  • Katres absent (volunteering with Austin Parks Foundation).
  • Finalize October events; FRFs created.
  • Rock the Pink FRF due October 7.
  • Cricuit tutorial idea proposed.
  • FRF for C3 supplies: toothbrushes, study materials, hair ties, fidgets.

October 10 (October 8 agenda)

  • Both senators and Katres absent that day; Rotem covered.
  • FRFs approved for October events.
  • New 85″ 4K Samsung TV ordered for TV Lounge.
  • Projector screen idea snagged on drilling approval - collapsible screen used in interim.
  • TV Lounge + Red River reserved for movie night; Plaza for costume contest.
  • Mica: HornsLink invites; check email for HornsLink invitation.
  • Day-of logistics for Rock the Pink due October 14.
  • Brainstormed Jester West basement access, decorating RA study rooms, Farmers Fridge in JE, UT Outpost outreach.

October 17

  • Budget increase of $159 announced.
  • Introductions: Kyle (URHA Board Buddy), Vince (Director of Sustainability).
  • Projector screen installation officially approved.
  • Discussion about Farmers Fridge in JE and the Patio Lounge access issue (no access after midnight for J-East).
  • Resource Handbook for residents - centralized resource list.
  • Residential Leadership Conference interest (Nov 21–23).
  • Día de los Muertos plan (set up ofrenda Oct 31, food/music night Nov 2nd).
  • Snoopy themed November boards; C3 PO vote scheduled next time.
  • Distribution started on TPC; need more liners/tampons/condoms.
  • Jester United Retreat that Sunday afternoon.

October 24

  • FRF for Day of the Dead approved by vote.
  • Projector screen here, can use for Coraline.
  • Proposal: weekly movie nights with free popcorn (popcorn machine) - “low cost/effort, effective community building.”
  • Movie scheduling: Nov 7 (Austin/Kayla - Wicked), Dec 5 (Mica/Zoe - KPDH or Christmas).
  • Idea floated: Little Free Library for C3 (“I know where we can get books! Just need a shelf/signage”).
  • Patio Lounge after-midnight access - declined.
  • Piano complaints (too loud, used during quiet hours): piano lock, doors, roping off proposed.
  • Trail of Lights and Jormal planning. Once vision/FRF in hand, request collaborative URHA funds (sooner the better).

October 31 (Halloween!)

  • Prodevo: claim UT email; Office365 management.
  • PS5 update - Jester West will maybe buy in March.
  • Resource booklet placed at front desks.
  • Reminder: event proposals must be in Roompact 10 days before event date.
  • Event proposal tutorial.
  • Rock the Pink retro: “Residents liked it, good turnout, good messaging, compliments from peers, amazing banner.”
  • Brainstorming East vs West field-day competition (sports, trivia, dance-off, Olympics tournament).
  • Día de los Muertos Sunday - set up ofrenda today.

November 7

  • Educational component to movie nights (“university under scrutiny rn, looking for excuses to pull $$”).
  • Door for piano area - proposal being written.
  • Little Library Bookshelf is here, just needs to be built.
  • Trail of Lights orders mostly arrived.
  • Wicked Movie Night setup volunteers: Kayla, Katres, Austin, Erica, Rayyan, Maggie.
  • KPDH movie night next week - HEB run planned.

November 14

  • Ad-hoc election for Director of Outreach scheduled Dec 5 (Keira congratulated).
  • Multiple OTM congratulations (Erica + others).
  • Name tags ordered.
  • URHA E-board interest discussion.
  • T-shirt design competition preliminary results.
  • Trail of Lights scheduling.
  • Daily Texan article reference.
  • Jormal update.
  • Senator updates: Leadership Development / Longhorns Listen - Suicide Prevention Training Tuesday Nov 18 at Jester Fireplace Lounge (dinner provided).
  • Programming: Ramyeon and Demon Hunters tonight; Fuel for Finals on Monday; Cookie Decor; Colors and Connections coming up.

December 5 (final fall meeting)

  • Trail of Lights yesterday - “important folks in UHD” stopped by impressed.
  • Jormal: Enchanted Forest theme proposal fully approved up to director level; Mica and Manoj presented to URHA Eboard and got funding.
  • Resource room expectations reiterated: never alone, basic upkeep, no event-supply storage there, ask Mireya for refills, use only when actively using items, not for studying or lounging. Looking for additional spaces for JE RHC.
  • Roompact access issue - IT working on resolution.
  • Thang departing for UTLA opportunity in spring (congrats!).
  • Whoville and Hot Cocoa movie tonight; Ornament Decorating on Monday.

Spring 2026 - Meeting-by-Meeting Highlights

January 22 - Spring kickoff

  • Jester East won an OTM award for Red River Cinema!
  • Jester Formal was a huge hit; first UHD event of 2026.
  • “One last mandatory-ish event left: Bunches of Love!”
  • Working on cleaner for popcorn machine.
  • HornsLink completed thanks to all.
  • URHA Exec Board Applications open; virtual info session Friday Jan 23.
  • Erica won an OTM at the national level - First Year Student of the Month!
  • Bunches of Love planning. Red River Cinema scheduling. Jedding - JW RHC working with us. East vs West Olympics? Other Feb event ideas: cookie decorating.
  • Floor reps Selena, Vince, Brooke, Rayyan, Layla - please add yourselves to events.
  • Katres: make a JEast RHC company listing on LinkedIn; check IT on Roompact access.

January 29

  • URHA wanted to host an OTM writing party on Egg Hunt day; Katres reserved space; URHA offered to help purchase decorations.
  • One event on calendar overlapped with movie night (a typo) - flagged in red.
  • Roompact access working for everyone except Navya.
  • Reminder: event proposal must be submitted 10 days before event.
  • Mica/Austin: Think about FRFs for movie nights; URHA Board elections open.
  • TPC: refill basket next week.
  • C3: next meeting Feb 2 at 5:30 (extended).
  • Programming: Twilight this Friday 6 p.m.
  • Tasks: Abhishek list URHA-funded supplies; Katres edit/reprint movie night flyer (two movies same date typo); confirm Jedding date by next meeting; FRFs/event forms to be filed by hosts.

February 5

  • Cookie decor items bought; flowers ordered for Bunches of Love.
  • Food, clothing, and book drives planned.
  • URHA Phase 2 applications due Feb 10. Panel: “From Imposter to Empowered” (TALPA) Feb 9.
  • Jedding planning meeting tomorrow 5–6 p.m.
  • Programming: 10 Things I Hate About U happening that night; Bunches of Love next Tuesday; Cookie Decor next Wednesday; Vince - food drive.

February 13

  • Great job at Bunches of Love and Baked with Love.
  • LLC Peer Leader hiring open for 2026–27 ($800 Dine-In Dollars/year).
  • URHA cool events coming up.
  • TPC: condoms needed; refill next week; aprons coming; bedazzling planned. Budget $200 every two weeks.
  • Programming: Scary Movie 4 tomorrow; Jedding details - East/West met Feb 6 to begin planning.
  • Jedding: Date TBD, aiming April 3 or 10. Theme: Royalty. Budget < $1,000 each. Phoenix costume sourced. UHD pasta catering quote. Photobooth frame from URHA. Erica designed sketch.

February 19

  • Constitution amendment proposed and ratified by majority secret-ballot vote: “Arriving more than 10 minutes late or leaving 10 minutes before the meeting is adjourned, without prior 24-hour notice, will count as 0.5 absences.”
  • Tips for improving flyers presentation (Katres’ powerpoint): simplicity, clear objective, date/time, post near elevators/doors/online/newsletters, monochrome, promote 3 weeks out.
  • TPC: refills complete; new baskets; website by Erica created (request forms, layout maps); Wednesdays 3 p.m.
  • Programming: Princess Bride tomorrow (Keira solo - help needed); Jedding meeting Feb 20, 5–6 p.m.

February 26

  • Welcomed RA Liaison Sam and Floor Reps Grant, Leon, Prisha.
  • Social Media + RA Social Media merger to JEast Social Media.
  • Lucky Charms event conflicting with MAR10 - push back?
  • RRC low-attendance concerns - “polling residents to gauge interest” idea.
  • NRHH applications due Mar 13.
  • URHA’s Stop, Drop, and Give Thanks next Thursday 12–4 p.m. (dogs, letters, fun!).
  • Adam (URHA board buddy) offered leadership development lessons at meetings.
  • Lucky Charms detailed planning (leprechaun theme, Irish music, beaded charms, Bevo template, gold coins, scavenger hunt). Confirmed: March 11, 6–7 p.m.
  • Spring Forward (Spring Forward come-and-go) moved to March 9, 7 a.m.
  • Twilight Marathon: April 7–10, 7:30 p.m. daily.
  • Katres reserved Red River Lounge.

March 5

  • Movie night IG poll up: Pitch Perfect and Rio tied.
  • $959 left for programming.
  • Reach out to William Sparks.
  • Jollapalooza on March 15 (RA-planned, between East and West).
  • Jedding chat: Mica creating chat. Date suggestion April 3. Casino Night URHA Wed Mar 11 (poker, roulette, blackjack; digital camera prize; wings).
  • TPC: refills finished; two new candidates; March 11 decorate aprons; condoms needed.
  • Spring Forward Monday morning. Lucky Charms next Wednesday. Egg Hunt collab with Adam.
  • Twilight Marathon: Erica reaching out to URHA. April 7–10, 7 p.m. daily. Raffle for T-shirts (“Bella, where have you been loca?”).

March 12

  • Name tags made (thanks Leon & Maggie). Available in office.
  • Quick vote ratified “statement of compliance” addition to constitution.
  • Jollapalooza April 16, 7–9 p.m. - volunteers needed.
  • Student Leadership Celebration April 14 at 6 p.m. - save the date.
  • URHA funding request planned March 30 or 23.
  • Spring Forward successful.
  • NRHH application due March 27.
  • Lucky Charms ate down.
  • Spring into the Szn after break (Katres, Alejandra, Navya, Carmen) Tuesday 6:30 p.m.
  • Egg Hunt after break (Abhishek, Carmen).
  • Sinners movie night today at 7.

March 26

  • Spring devo/retreat funds discussion: retreat? treat bags? East-only retreat? Junited retreat? Cidercade trip? Skip retreat and roll funds into programming or Student Leadership Celebration?
  • Food at last RHC meeting: Cabo Bobs (depending on $$); plan B HEB sushi/sandwiches.
  • Jedding date change April 3 → April 10 (approved).
  • URHA proposal for funds Wednesday.
  • Student Leader Celebration April 14 (6:30–8 p.m.) at San Jac MPR.
  • NRHH applications due tomorrow at 11:59 p.m.
  • Programming: Jollapalooza volunteer signup sheet from Katres. Jedding date update. Clothing Drive (Abhishek): St. Vincent Donation April 20–May 1, prizes for donating (plushies, snacks, VIP event pass). Twilight Wk status (Erica) - making each day special. Slime event (Austin/Zoe) replaces sustainability event.
  • Kudos: Alejandra for Spring into the Szn help; Abhishek/Erica/Leon/Carmen for Egg Hunt.
  • Exit ticket: nominate someone for Student Leadership Celebration.

April 2

  • Kaitlyn/Karina proxying as board buddy for the rest of the semester.
  • Jedding banner painted tomorrow at 12 p.m.
  • Jormal won Best Vibes!!
  • Wedding invites printed by Monday.
  • Newsletter out today; Jedding love story promoting soon.
  • C3 great update on board; TPC apron decorating next Wednesday.
  • Jedding Friday April 10 - sign up for shifts. Vince meet with Mark on altar moment. Floor reps disperse door flyers Monday. Banner created tomorrow at noon. Costume worked on when materials arrive.

April 9 (penultimate meeting)

  • No major advisor updates.
  • Student Leadership Celebration 4/14 6:30 p.m. San Jac MPR - please come, awards and food and fun.
  • TPC decorated aprons. Social Media: move-out themed TikToks (defrost fridge tutorial; ASMR slime making).
  • Day 3 of Twilight Marathon today. Jedding tomorrow! Business casual + name tag. Slime & Study next Wednesday 5:30–6:30 p.m. Red River Lounge. Jollapalooza next Thursday.

April 23 (final meeting)

  • End-of-year celebration.
  • Cabo Bob’s catered for the meeting.
  • Bevo plushies handed out to every council member.
  • RHC leadership certificates presented to every council member.

Partners, Recognition, & Future

Sponsors, awards, year-end notes, and recommendations for 2026–2027.

Sponsors and Campus Partners

Primary Funder University Housing and Dining (UHD)

Primary funder of the council through departmental funds - housing@austin.utexas.edu

Umbrella Org University Residence Hall Association (URHA)

Provided supplementary funding for some events along with advising assistance as the umbrella student organization where senators from all Hall Councils convene for weekly meetings. URHA@austin.utexas.edu

Movies Student Association for Accessible Media (UT SAAM)

Free source of all movies played at movie nights. Best contact - katresai@icloud.com

Wellness Sponsor MagicMind Nootropics

Provided $1200 worth of wellness shot products for the Community Corner - Contact through OnBrand

Wellness Sponsor Bloom Nutrition

Provided energy drinks and green wellness powder drink mix products for the Community Corner - Contact through OnBrand

Event Partner Campus UnLonely

Partnered with us to put on the Colors & Connection event. Contact through Instagram.

Health Resource UT Longhorn Wellness Center

Provided free condoms for TPC and C3, contact through department website

Athletics Longhorn Run Race Committee

JE RHC sponsored a small amount of goodies such as electrolytes and more for runners from Jester, in collaboration with the Longhorn Run who provided bananas.

Sibling Council Jester West Residence Hall Council

Partner for Fall Retreat, Trail of Lights, Jester Formal Enchanted Forest, and Jedding.

Awards

  • Red River Cinema (the Friday-night movie series) - won an OTM (Of the Month) award for Jester East.
  • Jester Formal (Jormal) - “Won Best Vibes!” at year-end.
  • Jormal - Recognized as the “first UHD event of 2026” because of advance fall planning.
  • Erica - won an OTM at the national level for First Year Student of the Month.
  • Multiple OTM congratulations for Erica and Austin.
  • Spring Forward - nominated for an OTM by Austin (April 2).
  • Professional Staff Member of the Month - Katres
  • Advisor of the Month - Katres
  • Professional Staff Advisor of the Year - Katres
  • Community of the Year - Won at SWACURH 2025

Major Operational Improvements & Changes

  • New 85-inch 4K Samsung TV installed in TV Lounge (Oct).
  • 120-inch motorized projector screen installed in Red River Lounge.
  • Centralized Resource Booklet created and placed at both Jester front desks.
  • LinkedIn JEast RHC listing.
  • Roompact access issues escalated to UHD IT.
  • Microsoft Teams adopted as primary communication channel for all committees.
  • Newsletter.

2026–2027 Recommendations

For the ACC

Remember to advocate for the following:

  • 3 RA Liaisons instead of 2: My highest recommendations are Jaylen, Keira, and Sam.
  • The President has been elected in Spring 2026, Kayla Bui. Remember to omit the president position from election processes as it has been filled. Karina has been informed of this.
  • Begin recruitment early, before even the first day of classes. Throughout the year, send a few recruitment emails to pack the council, the more the better! An example email sent resulted in 8 more members joining in the Spring.
  • Go to as many events as possible, and all council meetings. Connect with your RA Liaisons to make sure they are present for when you cannot be.
  • Get food for meetings! We have the budget for it, I regret not taking advantage of those funds. If it were me doing it again, I would cater a small meal for each meeting or even just pizza. Simply getting snacks would be a good idea as well.
  • Request funds for things in the Fall from William Sparks and URHA. Large scale programming should not come from the RHC budget unless you are not confident in your spending ability.
  • I used all our funding under the premise of redistributing to the students, whether that be in the form of food, giveaways, prizes, or just a good solid event. Think outside the box! Do we need a new TV? Should we build something or renovate? Is there an org that wants to do an event in Jester but can’t afford supplies? We can sponsor them!

For RHC

Y’all are going to do amazing! Kayla and Keira were on the council last year and Jaylen and Sam have been working with the council as well for quite some time. Feel free to reach out to me as well if you need anything.

If you ever need ANY advice...
✉

I have been involved with RHC in every capacity from member, to president, to RA Liaison, to Advisor. My email is katresai@icloud.com and my number is 442-799-8771 and I would love to hear from you if you need anything!!

Best of luck, Jeast on top!!

How to use this site: click an icon on the desktop to open a window. Each window has tabs at the top — click to switch sections. Drag windows by their title bars. Bookmark the desktop and you'll come back to where you left off.

I built this so you wouldn't have to start from a blank page like I did. Steal anything. Improve everything.

You’ve got this, Katresai, San Jacinto ACC, Jester ACC, Jester SRA, Jester RA, BRP President, BRP PR Representative, URHA Senator, LC3 Member, Farm Stand Market Staff, UHD Desk Assistant, along with other various committee assignments.
